title: Astrobotic says sale to Voyager will allow it to scale up
summary: Astrobotic will sell to Voyager Technologies to accelerate its growth and meet NASA's lunar base initiative demands. The acquisition aims to help Astrobotic scale up rapidly for upcoming lunar missions.

title: India’s Jio lays out sovereign LEO constellation plan ahead of IPO
summary: Jio Platforms, India's largest telecom company, plans to lease broadband capacity from satellite constellations to build a sovereign LEO network ahead of its IPO. The move aims to establish a domestic low Earth orbit satellite network for broadband services.

title: MDA Space to buy Blue Canyon Technologies to gain foothold in U.S. market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring smallsat manufacturer Blue Canyon Technologies for $620 million to expand its presence in the U.S. government market. The deal aims to strengthen MDA Space's position in the lucrative U.S. space industry.

title: ElevationSpace Secures US $40 Million in Series B Funding, Bringing Total Raised to US $63.5 Million
summary: ElevationSpace Inc. raised an additional US $40 million in Series B funding, bringing its total funding to US $63.5 million. The company focuses on space-to-Earth transportation and space environment utilization platforms.

title: Northrop Grumman says industry ready to scale solid rocket production, with longer contracts
summary: Northrop Grumman states the space industry is prepared to expand solid rocket production, contingent on longer contracts. The company highlights the need for predictable demand from suppliers despite billions in private investment.

title: The MEO durability crisis: why LEO hardware will fail the new orbital economy
summary: The article discusses the challenges of Medium Earth Orbit (MEO) durability compared to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) hardware, highlighting why LEO systems may not meet the demands of the evolving orbital economy.

title: NASA selects mission to study space weather interaction with Earth’s atmosphere
summary: NASA has selected a new space science mission to investigate how space weather affects Earth's atmosphere. The mission aims to enhance understanding of the interactions between solar activity and atmospheric dynamics.

title: Chinese university-led mission to study asteroid Apophis during close encounter with Earth
summary: A spacecraft developed by Tsinghua University will participate in international efforts to study asteroid Apophis during its close approach to Earth in 2029. This mission aims to gather data on the asteroid's characteristics and behavior.

title: Mu-g Technologies enters the parabolic flight business
summary: Mu-g Technologies has acquired an aircraft to offer commercial parabolic flight services. This move comes as NASA seeks to acquire its own aircraft for reduced-gravity research.

title: Austrian propulsion startup joins sovereign space funding surge
summary: Austrian satellite propulsion startup Gate Space secured 6.3 million euros in funding from Europe's government-backed accelerator program to boost space sovereignty. The investment reflects a broader trend of European companies attracting capital for sovereign space capabilities.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 215 — Disclosure Day
summary: Episode 215 of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with Dr. David Brin, a bestselling sci-fi/sci-fact author, focusing on topics related to space and science. The episode is co-hosted by Rod Pyle and Susan Karlin.

title: Supermassive black holes may be surrounded by dark matter clusters, new 'echo map' technique suggests
summary: A new 'echo map' technique suggests supermassive black holes, including the one at the center of the Milky Way, may be surrounded by clusters of dark matter. This discovery could provide insights into the mysterious nature of dark matter and its interactions with black holes.

title: Late, great singer-songwriter Elliott Smith finds immortality as an asteroid
summary: An asteroid has been named after late singer-songwriter Elliott Smith, who passed away in 2003 at the age of 34. This posthumous honor immortalizes him in the cosmos.

title: Do we need a lunar building code to build moon bases safely?
summary: NASA is advancing plans for a sustained human presence on the moon, and an expert suggests the need for a lunar building code to ensure safe construction of moon bases.

title: A private company will build and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 — and it's not SpaceX
summary: A California-based private company will design, build, and launch NASA's next Mars orbiter in 2028 for the Aeolus mission, marking the first spacecraft to provide daily global environmental measurements of Mars. This contract excludes SpaceX.

title: 'This is not like life that we’ve experienced' 'Star City' cast on struggling to relate to the brutality of Soviet existence in Apple TV's For All Mankind' spin-off
summary: Apple TV+'s spin-off 'Star City' explores the harsh realities of Soviet life, with cast members expressing difficulty relating to the brutality depicted. The show contrasts this with the familiar experiences of American astronauts.

title: 'No one thought it was going to be possible.' A space telescope is falling out of space. This is NASA's daring plan to save it.
summary: NASA's Swift space observatory is falling out of orbit, and the agency is exploring a commercial solution to rescue it within nine months. A private company may develop a spacecraft to extend the telescope's mission.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is preparing for repairs to the Canadarm2 robotic arm following the departure of the CRS-34 mission. The repairs are part of ongoing maintenance to ensure the arm's functionality for future operations.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space has transported the second stage of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket to Stennis Space Center, marking progress toward its planned 2026 debut. The company continues advancing development of the fully reusable vehicle.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten space launches are scheduled worldwide in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches are expected to take place from various sites across the globe.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. Flight 12 was completed about three weeks ago.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, marking a significant milestone in its space program. LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. This marks a significant milestone for Japan's space program after previous setbacks.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has successfully completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: Artemis III Crew Named: Veteran Astronauts to test Lunar Landers in LEO
summary: NASA has named the four astronauts who will fly the Artemis III mission to test lunar landers in low Earth orbit. This crewed mission marks a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: Booster 20 into Cryogenic Testing ahead of Starship Flight 13
summary: SpaceX's Starbase team has begun cryogenic testing for Booster 20 in preparation for Starship Flight 13. This follows the recent conclusion of Starship Flight 12.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news topic. No specific details about the news content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm is being developed to enhance space operations, potentially revolutionizing satellite servicing and maintenance. The innovation aims to improve efficiency and reduce costs in the space industry.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks the resumption of routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on June 2 in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on their innovative ideas to ensure mission success.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions and outreach.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive space industry component. The image associated with the news is not described in detail.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a spacecraft parachute system, referred to as a 'bake-out.' This process likely involves heating the parachute to remove moisture or contaminants.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated high-fidelity quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its Q4S quantum networking satellite. The company plans to launch the satellite in 2027.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3 for its autonomous orbital vehicle, Jackal, meeting all test objectives and achieving full commissioning readiness.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space is shifting its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly participate in lunar surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies has raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans to launch a mission closer to Earth this month, followed by its first GEO mission in 2027.

title: Dawn Aerospace Raises $25M Series B
summary: Dawn Aerospace secured $25 million in a Series B funding round to accelerate the development of its Aurora spaceplane and conduct an in-space refueling demonstration.

title: Bill Spotlight: NEW HORIZON Act
summary: A bipartisan bill called the NEW HORIZON Act was introduced to allow national security missions to utilize commercial orbital data centers. The legislation aims to leverage the growing trend of commercial space-based data storage.

title: Optera Raises £3M to Open New UK HQ
summary: Optera raised £3 million to establish a new UK headquarters, aligning with the UK government's push to enhance sovereign technical capabilities.

title: PiLogic Partners with AFRL on AI Anomaly Detection Tech
summary: PiLogic has partnered with AFRL to develop AI anomaly detection technology, currently testing it on terrestrial flat sats with plans to expand into orbit.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: SpaceX's successful IPO led to a dip in stocks of other publicly traded space companies, causing major players to decline. Investors pulled back from the broader space sector following the strong debut.

title: JAXA Nails H3 Return to Flight
summary: JAXA successfully launched the H3 rocket on its eighth mission, marking its first successful flight with a new three-engine variant. The rocket delivered all six of its payloads to space from Japan's Tanegashima Space Center.

title: Quantum Space to Demonstrate Orbital Refueling Under Pentagon Contract
summary: Quantum Space will demonstrate orbital refueling using its Ranger spacecraft under a Pentagon contract awarded on June 18. The company aims to showcase in-space servicing capabilities for national security applications.

title: Iceye Data to Support Flood Monitoring in Norway
summary: The Norwegian Water Resources and Energy Directorate (NVE) has signed a deal with Iceye to use its satellite data for flood monitoring. This partnership aims to enhance Norway's disaster response and water resource management capabilities.

title: Vantor and Rheinmetall Plan Joint Venture for Sovereign Space in Germany
summary: Vantor and Rheinmetall have announced plans to form a joint venture aimed at developing sovereign space capabilities in Germany and Europe. The collaboration was formalized through a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on June.

title: Private Equity Firm EQT to Acquire Rideshare Provider Exolaunch
summary: Private equity firm EQT is acquiring rideshare provider Exolaunch, marking EQT's first investment in the space sector. The acquisition was announced on June 18 by Stockholm-based EQT.

title: Intellian Calls New Production Facility in California a ‘Defining Milestone’
summary: Intellian Technologies is opening a new large-scale manufacturing facility in California, marking a significant milestone for the company. The facility is described as a 'defining milestone' by CEO Eric Sung.

title: Lyntris Acquires Robotics Firm Novium to Further Satellite Offerings
summary: Lyntris, a newly formed defense tech company, acquired Houston-based robotics firm Novium to expand its satellite capabilities. The acquisition follows Lyntris's recent formation through the merger of Vitesse.

title: Bezos Earth Fund Invests in FireSat Constellation Build-Out
summary: The Bezos Earth Fund, led by Jeff Bezos, is investing $26 million to support the development of the FireSat constellation, a wildfire-monitoring satellite network. The investment is part of a broader coalition backing the non-profit Earth Fire Alliance.

title: Thursday Morning Conversation With Tina Ghataore
summary: The article features an interview with Tina Ghataore, a former Satellite Executive of the Year, discussing future developments in the satellite industry. It also mentions some breaking news related to the conversation.

title: Gitai Delays Robotic Servicing Mission to Prioritize SBI Work
summary: Gitai has completed a flight model for a robotic on-orbit servicing mission but is delaying the mission to prioritize space-based interceptor (SBI) technologies following a recent U.S. Space policy shift.

title: DHS Promotes Latest Space Cybersecurity Research in SPARTA
summary: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Science and Technology Directorate (S&T) unveiled its latest space cybersecurity research aimed at safeguarding critical infrastructure. The research is part of the Aerospace SPARTA framework.
